zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Cryspin於1220年出生在英國戴文時，他的父親William Crispyn21歲，母親Maud21歲。他於大約1250年與Arundella de Arundel在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1291年之前。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Cryspin于1220年出生在英国戴文时，他的父亲William Crispyn21岁，母亲Maud21岁。他于大约1250年与Arundella de Arundel在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1291年之前。

nameMeanings.name: Cryspin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Pedro, Alicia, Fernando, Manuel, Rafael, Adolfo, Alfredo, Ana, Areli, Candita.  French Adrien, Andre, Eugenie, Francoise.

English; Spanish (Crispín): from the personal name Middle English Crispin, Spanish Crispín, from Latin Crispinus, derived from crispus ‘curly-haired’ (see Crisp ). This name was especially popular in France in the early Middle Ages, having been borne by a Christian saint who was martyred at Soissons in AD c. 285 along with a companion, Crispinianus (whose name is a further derivative of the same word). Compare Crespin and 3 below.

English (of Norman origin): from Old French and Middle English crespin, crispin ‘curly-haired’, also found as Crepin; see Crippin . It is difficult to tell this apart from the personal name in 1 above. According to Lanfranc, archbishop of Canterbury (1070–1089), the Norman Gilbert Crispin was the first man to receive this nickname, and two of his sons adopted it as their surname. His grandson Gilbert Crispin became abbot of Westminster in 1085. Members of the Norman family were said to have inherited a shock of hair that stood up stiffly like a bristly pine, as though the name derived from Latin crispus pinus, but this derivation is a medieval invention.

variants.name: Crisp
meanings: English: nickname for a man with curly hair, from Middle English crisp, crips, scrip, Old English crisp, cryps (from Latin crispus), reinforced in Middle English by an Old French word also from Latin crispus. In some cases this is possibly a nickname from the personal name Crispin .
Americanized form of German Krisp: nickname from Middle High German krisp, krispel ‘curly-haired man’.
Americanized form of German Krisp: from a short form of the medieval personal name Krispin (see Crispin ).

variants.name: Crippen
meanings: English: variant of   Crispin  .
Irish: variant of   Gribbin  .

variants.name: Crespin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Raul, Vidal, Adelina, Alfonso, Amador, Andres, Aniceto, Delfino, Emilio.
Spanish (Crespín): variant of Crispin .
French: from the Latin personal name Crispinus (see Crispin ).

variants.name: Crispen
meanings: English: variant of   Crispin  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain.
Americanized form of German Krispin, a cognate of Crispin .

variants.name: Crispino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Luigi, Carlo, Amato, Angelo, Antonio, Domenico, Fiore, Gennaro, Michelina, Rocco, Sal, Salvator.
Italian (southern): from the personal name Crispino, from Latin Crispinus, a derivative of the old Roman surname Crispus ‘curly(-headed)’ (see Crispin ).

variants.name: Crippin
meanings: English: variant of   Crispin  .
Irish: variant of   Gribbin  .

variants.name: Crespi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jaime, Juan, Mercedes, Racquel, Alberto, Alejandro, Consuelo, Eduardo, Jose, Jose Manuel, Migdalia, Pedro.  Jewish Aron, Nissim.
Catalan (Crespí): from a personal name, the Catalan equivalent of Crispin .
Italian (northern): variant of   Crespo  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Cryspin was born in 1220, in Devon, England, his father, William Crispyn, was 21 and his mother, Maud, was 21. He married Arundella de Arundel about 1250, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died before 1291.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Cryspin est né en 1220, à Devon, Angleterre, son père, William Crispyn, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Maud, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Arundella de Arundel environ 1250, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé avant 1291.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Cryspin 1220 in Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Crispyn 21 und seine Mutter Maud 21. Er hat um 1250 in England Arundella de Arundel geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb vor 1291.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Cryspin è nato il nel 1220 nel Devon, England, il suo padre, William Crispyn, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Maud, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Arundella de Arundel circa 1250, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì prima del 1291.

ja.Devonshire,, England: イギリスイギリスデボン
ja.lifeSummary: Richard Cryspinが1220年にイギリスデボンで生まれた時，父William Crispynは21歳，母Maudは21歳であった。約1250年にイギリスでArundella de Arundelと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1291年以前に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Cryspin(이)가 1220년 영국 데폰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Crispyn(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1250년 영국에서 Arundella de Arundel(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1291년 이전 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Cryspin nasceu em 1220, em Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Crispyn, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Maud, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Arundella de Arundel aproximadamente 1250, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu antes 1291.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Cryspin родился в 1220 г. в местности девон, Англия, его отцу по имени William Crispyn было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Maud, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Arundella de Arundel около 1250 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер перед 1291 г.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Cryspin nació en 1220, en Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, William Crispyn, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Maud, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Arundella de Arundel aproximadamente 1250, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió antes de 1291.
